question.text: Common purposes that can use currency futures for hedging include:

answer.text: All of the above — imports/exports, foreign education fees, overseas medical treatment, loan repayments
Only loan repayments in foreign currency
Only investment in assets outside India

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Currency futures can hedge: imports/exports, travel expenses, education fees abroad, medical treatment abroad, employee payments abroad, foreign loans, overseas investment, and repatriation. **The Trap:** Currency hedging is not limited to trade transactions — any cross-border cash flow creates hedgeable currency exposure. **Cross-Exam Context:** Use cases for hedging are listed in the workbook. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Even individuals paying education fees or loan EMIs in foreign currency can use exchange-traded currency futures for hedging.
